question: The chart shows time allocation on a typical day. Which statement is correct according to the chart data?

options.A: Work takes up the largest share of time.
options.B: Sleep takes up more time than any other single activity.
options.C: Chores is the smallest category by percentage.
options.D: Leisure and Commute together account for more time than Work alone.

explanation: This pie chart uses dual encoding: slice angle represents percentage, and the radius also varies with percentage, so slice area differences are exaggerated. That can make the largest category look overly dominant. Work: 35%; Sleep: 25%; Leisure: 20%; Commute: 10%; Chores: 10%.
